calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-31-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for and with (executor) Barbara Simon.

The Court addressed the cash in the Schwab accounts, asked if there were any concerns and asked about getting it into FDIC covered accounts. Ms. Simon reported that Schwab has 3 levels of insurance, that not all are FDIC, that one of the levels covers $500,000.00 and that Lloyds of London provides $1,500,000.00 in coverage, that not 100% of the cash was in and out of CD's, that within 30 days of receipt of funds from the sale they were rolling in and out of CD's with different banks that had FDIC coverage and that she thought they already had ample coverage. The Court asked about the construction bid. Ms. Simon explained that she had multiple jobs going on at the same time, that she had 4 flooring and 4 painting bids, that the bid for flooring was significantly less than the other contractor she was using, that the painting bid was within $500.00 of the other painting bids and included removing wallpaper and that the concern was getting the house on the market before the summer rush. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)
